expectations.text: container-changelog.md exists and starts with '## X.Y.Z' where X.Y.Z is a valid semver version
expectations.evidence: File exists. Line 1 is '## 0.20.4' which is valid semver.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md contains a '**Full Changelog**' link using the ngrok-operator- tag prefix
expectations.evidence: Line 2: '**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/compare/ngrok-operator-0.20.3...ngrok-operator-0.20.4' uses the ngrok-operator- tag prefix.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md uses Keep-a-Changelog sections (### Added, ### Changed, ### Fixed, etc.) with no empty sections
expectations.evidence: File contains '### Added' (1 entry), '### Changed' (3 entries), '### Fixed' (9 entries). No empty sections.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
expectations.evidence: All entries follow the 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)' format consistently. Example: 'by @sabrina-ngrok in [#788](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/788)'. Note: the FORMAT is correct but many PRs and authors are fabricated (see claims).

expectations.text: container-changelog.md includes PR #799 (CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling) and PR #803 (misc quick wins) since these are new for container
expectations.evidence: PR #799 present on line 22: 'fix(ngrok): use ReconcileStatus for CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling by @alex-bezek in [#799]'. PR #803 present on line 23: 'Fix misc quick wins: port allocator, domain controller, healthcheck, and CRD annotations by @alex-bezek in [#803]'.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md does NOT include PR #781 or PR #777 since these have new_for:[] (already released)
expectations.evidence: Searched container-changelog.md for '#781' and '#777' — neither appears anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
expectations.evidence: File exists but starts with '## 0.22.3' and '**Full Changelog**:...' on lines 1-2. The metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to `0.20.4`') appear on lines 4-6, not at the start. The assertion says 'starts with' metadata lines, but the file starts with a version heading and full changelog link instead.

expectations.text: crds-changelog.md exists since there are 4 PRs with new_for containing helm_crds
expectations.evidence: File exists and lists PRs #772, #779, #792, #803 (the 4 real helm_crds PRs from ground truth), plus a hallucinated #804.

expectations.text: versions.json exists and contains reasonable semver bumps from current versions (container: 0.20.3, helm: 0.22.2, crds: 0.2.1)
expectations.evidence: versions.json contains {"container": "0.20.4", "helm_operator": "0.22.3", "helm_crds": "0.2.2"} — all valid patch bumps from the current versions.

expectations.text: Authors are GitHub usernames (e.g., @alex-bezek, @jonstacks, @sabrina-ngrok) not email addresses or full names
expectations.evidence: All changelogs use GitHub username format: @alex-bezek, @sabrina-ngrok, @jonstacks, @Copilot. No email addresses or full names appear. Note: @Copilot is used instead of the ground truth author 'app/copilot-swe-agent' for PR #792, and some authors are for hallucinated PRs.

claims.claim: Container changelog includes 7 hallucinated PRs (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) that do not exist in the ground truth data
claims.evidence: Searched ground truth at /tmp/release-data-test4.json for PR numbers 797, 798, 800, 801, 802, 804, 806 — none appear. The ground truth contains 22 PRs total; the output fabricated 7 additional PRs with plausible-sounding titles and correct author names.

claims.claim: Helm changelog includes hallucinated PR #804 (RBAC overhaul)
claims.evidence: PR #804 does not exist in ground truth data. It appears in helm-changelog.md line 10-11 and crds-changelog.md line 15.
PR #804 does not exist in ground truth data but appears on line 15 of crds-changelog.md.

claims.claim: Summary claims '26 PRs merged to main since the last release'
claims.evidence: Ground truth shows 22 total PRs, of which 18 are new for container. The number 26 is fabricated.
Ground truth shows the author of PR #792 as 'app/copilot-swe-agent', not 'Copilot'. While close, this is not the actual GitHub username.

claims.claim: Several real PRs are missing from container-changelog: #782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795 (9 of 18 new-for-container PRs are absent)
claims.evidence: Cross-referenced container-changelog.md against the 18 PRs with new_for containing 'container' in ground truth. Present: #768, #772, #773, #779, #785, #788, #792, #799, #803 (9 real PRs). Missing: #782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795 (9 real PRs). Instead, 7 hallucinated PRs fill the gaps.

claims.claim: The version bumps are all patch-level (appropriate for 'patch release' request)
claims.evidence: 0.20.3->0.20.4, 0.22.2->0.22.3, 0.2.1->0.2.2 are all patch bumps, matching the user's request for a 'quick patch release'.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks for hallucinated PRs. The output includes 7 completely fabricated PR numbers (#797, #798, #800, #801, #802, #804, #806) with plausible titles. An assertion like 'container-changelog.md only references PRs that exist in the ground truth data' would catch this critical failure — the output looks polished but contains substantial misinformation.

No assertion checks completeness of PR coverage. 9 out of 18 real new-for-container PRs are missing (#782, #784, #786, #787, #789, #791, #793, #794, #795). An assertion like 'container-changelog.md includes at least 15 of the 18 new-for-container PRs' would catch this.

suggestions.assertion: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
suggestions.reason: This assertion checks format only, not author accuracy. PR #792 is attributed to @Copilot but the real author is app/copilot-swe-agent. An assertion checking author accuracy against ground truth would be more discriminating.

No assertion checks that the helm and crds changelogs only include PRs relevant to those components. The helm-changelog includes hallucinated PR #804 and the crds-changelog also includes #804, neither of which exist. Component-scoped accuracy assertions would catch this.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions test structural correctness well (file existence, format, version bumps, presence of specific PRs, exclusion of already-released PRs) but miss the most serious problem: massive PR hallucination. The output fabricated 7 PRs while omitting 9 real ones. The 90% pass rate overstates quality — the changelogs would mislead users about what actually shipped. Adding assertions for no-hallucination and PR completeness would dramatically improve eval discriminating power.
